title: Section 20
content: 1* * * Every proprietor entitled to vote at any general meeting may give a proxy in writing, general or special, limited or unlimited, and signed by himself or by his attorney duly authorized thereunto, to any other proprietor; and 2* * * the proprietor to whom the proxy is given, may vote on behalf of the proprietor who had given the proxy, according to the terms of such proxy.
footnote: 1. The words “And it is hereby enacted, that” omitted by Act 12 of 1891, s. 2 and Sch. I. 2. The word “that” omitted by s. 2 and Sch. I, ibid.
